outlook.headline: Sovereign AI demand is broadening from early-stage to meaningful
outlook.summary: Sovereign AI demand looks to be improving over the next 1-2 quarters: all reads are bullish, and multiple companies point to expanding customer breadth beyond a single vendor or node in the stack. NVIDIA says sovereign revenue is growing very fast and becoming meaningful, while AMD, Qualcomm, Astera Labs, Credo, ADI, and Seagate all describe widening opportunity pipelines or incremental demand from sovereign and neo-cloud builds. Mentions are still relatively low, but the recent uptick to 0.2x and slight momentum above trend suggest the theme is early but building.

drivers.label: Sovereign demand becoming meaningful
drivers.detail: Sovereign revenue is growing very fast and is now a meaningful demand vector.

drivers.label: Broader accelerator adoption
drivers.detail: Accelerator adoption is expanding across sovereign customers, increasing demand breadth.

drivers.label: Security and sovereignty concerns
drivers.detail: National sovereignty concerns are accelerating an already strong growth path.

drivers.label: Pipeline expansion in sovereign AI
drivers.detail: Multiple sovereign AI opportunities point to an expanding demand pipeline.

drivers.label: Early build-out plus neo-cloud pull
drivers.detail: Sovereign entities are still early in build-out and are signaling continued AI infrastructure demand, including from neo-clouds.

risks: Mentions are still low, so demand may remain early-stage despite the uptick.
Sovereign build-outs may take longer to convert into large near-term revenue than the bullish commentary implies.
If the current momentum fades, the theme could revert from broadening to merely incremental.
